entries.source: FAO Southeast Asian home garden traditions; UF/IFAS Tropical Research

entries.description: Andean traditional intercrop: rocoto pepper with huacatay marigold. The Tagetes minuta root exudates are strongly nematode-suppressive in the Andean cool soils where rocoto thrives.
entries.source: International Potato Center (CIP) Andean intercropping research; Native Seeds/SEARCH

entries.description: Nasturtium pulls aphids and cucumber beetles off the cucumber vines; sacrifice the nasturtium and prune off heavily-infested flowers. Cucumber yield protected through midsummer.
entries.source: USDA-ARS cucumber beetle trap crop research; Xerces Society

entries.description: Japanese / Korean traditional intercrop; aromatic shiso oils repel flea beetle and cucumber beetle near long-vining Asian cucumber cultivars.
entries.source: Kitazawa Seed Company variety notes; FAO Japanese home garden traditions

entries.description: Dill's umbel flowers attract predatory wasps and hoverflies that feed on cucumber aphids; dill also attracts swallowtail butterflies. Let some dill plants flower for full effect.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications; Cunningham 'Great Garden Companions'

entries.description: Empress of India nasturtium near delicata vines pulls squash bugs and cucumber beetles. The nasturtium also attracts pollinators that improve squash fruit set.
entries.source: USDA-ARS cucurbit IPM research; Xerces Society

entries.description: Borage flowers attract honeybees and bumblebees through the morning before kabocha flowers open, then bees flow to the squash flowers. Improves squash fruit set in cool maritime climates.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications; Cornell maritime cucurbit notes

entries.description: French marigold root exudates suppress soil nematodes that attack Cucurbita pepo roots; plant alongside squash hills at sowing.
entries.source: USDA-ARS Meloidogyne nematode research; UF/IFAS cucurbit IPM

entries.description: Nasturtium attracts cucumber beetles away from watermelon vines during the critical 4-week post-transplant window when bacterial wilt vector pressure peaks.
entries.source: UMass Vegetable Program cucurbit IPM; Cunningham 'Great Garden Companions'

entries.description: Borage attracts wild bumblebees that pollinate melon flowers; critical for the short-flowering Charentais cultivar which sets only morning-pollinated fruit.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications; UC ANR melon notes

entries.description: Summer savory (bohnenkraut) traditional German bean companion; aromatic terpenes deter Mexican bean beetle. Also flavors the cooked beans.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'; German traditional companion records

entries.description: Winter savory's stronger essential oil concentration provides season-long bean beetle deterrence; perennial habit means re-planting unnecessary.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'; Cornell perennial herb notes

entries.description: Sonoran traditional intercrop: heat-adapted tepary bean shades soil for amaranth seedlings; amaranth deep taproot mines minerals beans deposit through Rhizobium nodules.
entries.source: Native Seeds/SEARCH Sonoran traditional agriculture; Tohono O'odham intercrop records

entries.description: Carrots planted between rows of dry bush beans share the soil profile; carrots use the upper 12 inches that beans leave free.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'; Cornell vegetable companion notes

entries.description: Russian comfrey planted in the apple drip line; deep taproot mines potassium and calcium from subsoil, leaves chop-and-drop into the orchard floor mulch. The defining dynamic accumulator for apple guilds.
entries.source: Permaculture Designers Manual - Bill Mollison; Dave Jacke 'Edible Forest Gardens'

entries.description: Yarrow's umbel flowers attract parasitic wasps and lacewings that prey on apple aphid; mulch yarrow chop-and-drop also provides accumulator minerals. Plant beneath apple drip line.
entries.source: Xerces Society orchard pollinator plants; USDA-ARS apple orchard floor research

entries.description: Chive allyl-sulfur volatiles reduce apple scab spore germination on leaves; chives planted in orchard floor reduce disease pressure. Also attract pollinators when allowed to bloom.
entries.source: USDA-ARS apple scab management; Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: German chamomile flowers attract hoverflies and lacewings that prey on pear psylla; the small flower clusters bloom in waves through summer matching psylla generations.
entries.source: Xerces Society pear IPM pollinator plants; Penn State Tree Fruit guide

entries.description: Calendula's open composite flowers attract hoverflies that lay eggs on cherry aphid; the larvae consume aphids through the bloom-to-fruit critical window.
entries.source: Xerces Society; Cornell cherry IPM guide

entries.description: Tansy planted at the base of peach trees; volatile thujone and parthenolide deter peach tree borer larvae. Replace every 3 to 4 years as tansy can become invasive.
entries.source: Cornell Tree Fruit IPM peach borer management; Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: Garlic planted under plum trees; allyl-sulfur volatiles deter plum curculio and reduce brown rot spore viability on adjacent leaves.
entries.source: Cornell Tree Fruit IPM plum curculio management

entries.description: Rosemary's winter-early-spring flowers feed solitary bees that emerge before fig pollinators; supports the pollinator community that visits fig flowers when they open.
entries.source: Xerces Society winter-blooming pollinator plants; UC ANR fig orchard guide

entries.description: Comfrey planted at the blueberry drip line; deep taproot tolerates acidic blueberry soil while accumulating potassium for the shallow blueberry roots. Chop-and-drop mulch suppresses weeds.
entries.source: Dave Jacke 'Edible Forest Gardens'; Cornell blueberry orchard floor guide

entries.description: Borage interplanted in strawberry beds; the morning-pollinator visits to borage flow to strawberry flowers, improving fruit set and berry size. The classic Riotte pairing.
entries.source: Xerces Society strawberry pollinator research; Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: Pennyroyal planted at strawberry bed edges; pulegone volatiles repel ants that farm aphids on strawberry foliage. WARNING: pennyroyal is toxic to pets if ingested.
entries.source: Cornell strawberry IPM guide; Cunningham 'Great Garden Companions'

entries.description: Yarrow planted between raspberry rows; the flat umbel flowers attract aphid-predator hoverflies and tachinid wasps that prey on raspberry cane borer.
entries.source: Xerces Society raspberry pollinator plants; Cornell raspberry IPM

entries.description: Buttercrunch lettuce shades shallow carrot roots through the heat of midsummer; lettuce harvested before carrots size up. Classic interbed succession.
entries.source: Cornell vegetable companion notes; Cunningham 'Great Garden Companions'

entries.description: Radish row-marker between lettuce rows; radish matures (30 days) before lettuce needs the space (50 days). Standard intensive-bed pairing.
entries.source: Cornell vegetable companion notes; Johnny's Selected Seeds intensive-bed planning

entries.description: Chives planted alongside carrot rows; allyl-sulfur volatiles repel carrot rust fly. Standard British / European traditional carrot bed pairing.
entries.source: Royal Horticultural Society carrot pest management; Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: Horseradish planted at corners of potato beds; the volatile isothiocyanates deter potato beetle and Colorado potato beetle. Pot up horseradish at season end to control its spread.
entries.source: Cornell potato IPM; Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: French marigold root exudates suppress potato root-knot nematode populations; companion plant at the edge of potato hills.
entries.source: USDA-ARS Meloidogyne nematode research; Cornell potato IPM guide

entries.description: Onion and carrot rotated in adjacent rows; carrot rust fly repelled by onion volatiles, onion fly repelled by carrot volatiles. The classic Northeast European pairing.
entries.source: Royal Horticultural Society onion-carrot intercrop research

entries.description: Garlic planted at strawberry bed corners; allyl-sulfur volatiles deter strawberry root weevil and gray mold spore activity. Strawberry pickers report easier-to-spot berries with garlic borders.
entries.source: Cornell strawberry IPM guide; OSU strawberry bed management notes

entries.description: Both Solanaceae; share early blight (Alternaria solani), late blight (Phytophthora infestans), and bacterial speck (Pseudomonas) pathogens. Rotate beds annually; do not interplant.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension Solanaceae rotation guide; USDA-ARS

entries.description: Tomato and potato both host Phytophthora infestans (late blight). Do not interplant or rotate within 2 years; one infected crop quickly seeds the other.
entries.source: USDA-ARS late blight research; Cornell Cooperative Extension Solanaceae guide

entries.description: Allium root exudates inhibit Rhizobium leguminosarum nitrogen-fixation symbiosis in beans. Yields drop 30 to 50 percent when interplanted closer than 18 inches.
entries.source: USDA-ARS Rhizobium / Allium allelopathy research

entries.description: Garlic suppresses Rhizobium leguminosarum nitrogen-fixation in pole beans; the further from garlic, the better bean yield. The classic allium-legume incompatibility.
entries.source: USDA-ARS Rhizobium / Allium allelopathy research; Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: Fennel root exudates inhibit most garden vegetables including tomato; widely cited as the most antagonistic culinary vegetable. Plant fennel in its own bed.
entries.source: USDA-ARS allelopathy publications; Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: Fennel and dill cross-pollinate and produce sterile bitter seed; even isolated they suppress each other's growth via root exudate competition.
entries.source: Cornell vegetable variety database; UK Royal Horticultural Society fennel notes

entries.description: Reverse-direction: tomato yields suppressed when grown within 4 ft of fennel due to fennel root exudate effect on tomato hormone signaling.
entries.source: USDA-ARS allelopathy publications

entries.description: Lupine fixes nitrogen and tolerates the acidic soil blueberry requires; companion-plant in the orchard floor. Few other N-fixers tolerate blueberry pH 4.5 to 5.2.
entries.source: Dave Jacke 'Edible Forest Gardens'; Cornell blueberry orchard floor management

entries.description: Rosemary planted near cabbage; aromatic camphor and pinene confuse cabbage moth (Pieris rapae) flight orientation, reducing egg-laying. Use 2 to 3 rosemary plants per 10 ft of brassica row.
entries.source: Cornell cabbage IPM guide; Cunningham 'Great Garden Companions'

entries.description: Thyme planted between broccoli rows; thymol volatiles disrupt cabbage moth oviposition. Excellent perennial companion border for brassica beds.
entries.source: Cornell cabbage IPM guide; Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: Sage planted with kale; high-thujone volatiles deter cabbage moth and aphid colonization. Drier soil preferences both compatible.
entries.source: Cornell cabbage IPM guide; OSU Extension perennial herb companion notes

entries.description: Greek oregano allowed to flower beside pepper plants; tiny white flowers attract solitary bees and parasitic wasps that prey on pepper weevil.
entries.source: Xerces Society pepper pollinator plants; Cornell pepper IPM

entries.description: Buckwheat sown in strips between watermelon rows; abundant pale-white flowers attract honeybees and bumblebees during the critical mid-summer watermelon flowering window.
entries.source: USDA-ARS cucurbit pollinator research; Xerces Society

entries.description: Tall grain amaranth grown at row ends of sweet corn; uses field margins for grain harvest while corn fills the bed. Mesoamerican traditional intercrop.
entries.source: Native Seeds/SEARCH; Mesoamerican corn-amaranth intercrop records

entries.description: Andean traditional intercrop; fava bean fixes nitrogen for quinoa, quinoa shades fava through summer. Both bolt and senesce together.
entries.source: International Potato Center (CIP); Andean traditional intercrop records

entries.description: Southern traditional intercrop; cowpea fixes nitrogen for okra, okra provides high vertical shade tolerant cowpea needs. The defining Black American food garden pairing.
entries.source: Truelove Seeds southern heritage seed catalog; UF/IFAS southern intercrop notes

entries.description: Sweet potato vines spread as living mulch under pepper plants; suppresses weeds, retains soil moisture, and provides shade for pepper roots in hot summers.
entries.source: Truelove Seeds southern heritage seed catalog; UF/IFAS Extension

entries.description: Asian heat-loving intercrop; the trailing sweet potato vines shade soil for Thai pepper roots. Both crops need long warm seasons.
entries.source: UH Manoa Cooperative Extension; FAO Pacific tropical intercrop notes

entries.description: Basil planted near stevia; aromatic oils deter aphid colonization of stevia leaves. Both thrive in similar moist warm soil.
entries.source: UF/IFAS specialty herbs notes; Strictly Medicinal Seeds catalog

entries.description: Traditional Asian dual-rhizome cultivation; turmeric and ginger share light-shade, moist-soil, warm-night requirements. Plant in shared mulched beds with 18 inch spacing.
entries.source: UF/IFAS Extension; Indian Institute of Spices Research

entries.description: Andean traditional intercrop: huacatay (Tagetes minuta) at the corners of potato hills. Marigold-family nematode suppression in the Andean cool soils where the Inca developed both crops together.
entries.source: International Potato Center (CIP) Andean intercropping research; USDA-ARS Tagetes nematode suppression

entries.description: Mexican summer cilantro-substitute planted near beans; sulfur-rich volatiles deter Mexican bean beetle (Epilachna varivestis). The traditional Mesoamerican milpa intercrop.
entries.source: Native Seeds/SEARCH; Mesoamerican traditional agriculture records

entries.description: Mexican traditional pairing: epazote planted near pinto beans. Beyond garden companion, the dried epazote leaves are added to the bean pot to reduce gas production.
entries.source: Native Seeds/SEARCH; Mexican traditional cuisine records

entries.description: Thyme planted at pomegranate base; the aromatic groundcover deters Argentine ant trails that farm pomegranate aphids. Drought-tolerant pairing.
entries.source: UC ANR pomegranate IPM; Cornell perennial herb companion notes

entries.description: Yarrow beneath jujube; flat umbels attract honeybees during the brief jujube flowering window. Drought-tolerant pairing for arid orchards.
entries.source: USDA-ARS jujube cultivation notes; Xerces Society arid pollinator plants

entries.description: Asparagus fern provides ground-cover shade beneath fig trees; asparagus roots tolerate fig's competing surface roots. Both Mediterranean-climate adapted perennials.
entries.source: UC ANR fig orchard guide; Eric Toensmeier 'Perennial Vegetables'

entries.description: Saffron crocus planted between lavender rows; lavender shades the corms through summer dormancy, saffron blooms in fall when lavender dormant. Excellent Mediterranean-climate dual-crop bed.
entries.source: University of Vermont Saffron Project; Mediterranean cropping system records

entries.description: Establish comfrey in a 1 meter ring around Cosmic Crisp; cut the foliage to mulch the root zone and feed slow-release potassium that improves fruit storage life.
entries.source: WSU Tree Fruit Research; Toensmeier 'Edible Forest Gardens'

entries.description: Underplant Gala with sterile Bocking 14 comfrey; chop 3 times yearly to feed the tree and suppress orchard grass around the trunk.
entries.source: Permaculture Designers Manual - Bill Mollison

entries.description: Ring chives at the base of Honeycrisp; the sulfur compounds disrupt codling moth and apple scab spore germination on lower bark.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'; Cornell Cooperative Extension tree fruit IPM guide

entries.description: Plant a chive ring 30 cm from the Cosmic Crisp trunk; the volatile sulfides reduce aphid colonization and suppress scab on lower limbs.
entries.source: WSU Tree Fruit Research

entries.description: Chives planted at the Gala drip line repel woolly apple aphid and reduce codling moth egg laying via sulfur volatiles.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: Chives at the Pink Lady base release sulfur volatiles that deter codling moth and reduce woolly apple aphid colonies on lower bark.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: Plant a yarrow patch within 5 meters of Honeycrisp to draw hoverflies and parasitic wasps that prey on codling moth and aphids during bloom.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications; Cornell Cooperative Extension tree fruit IPM guide

entries.description: Yarrow planted in the Gala understory provides season-long nectar for parasitic wasps targeting leafrollers and aphids.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension tree fruit IPM guide

entries.description: Maintain a white-clover living mulch in the Honeycrisp row middles; the legume fixes 50 to 100 kg N per hectare per year and reduces fertilizer needs.
entries.source: USDA-ARS apple orchard floor management research

entries.description: White clover in Cosmic Crisp alleys supplies nitrogen, supports ground beetles, and reduces dust that hosts spider mites.
entries.source: WSU Tree Fruit Research; USDA-ARS orchard floor management

entries.description: A white-clover floor under Gala supplies nitrogen, shades weed seeds, and supports predator habitat for codling moth biocontrol.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension tree fruit IPM guide

entries.description: Plant a daffodil ring at the Honeycrisp drip line; the alkaloids in the bulbs deter voles and mice from girdling the trunk in winter.
entries.source: Toensmeier 'Edible Forest Gardens'; Cornell Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Daffodils planted in a ring around Cosmic Crisp deter voles and gophers while their early bloom feeds emerging mason bees.
entries.source: WSU Tree Fruit Research

entries.description: Plant 8 to 12 daffodil bulbs in a ring 50 cm from the Gala trunk to deter vole girdling without competing for water.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension tree fruit IPM guide

entries.description: Sow chamomile in the Bartlett understory; the bloom draws hoverflies and parasitic wasps that suppress pear psylla and codling moth.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'; Xerces Society pollinator publications

entries.description: Plant chamomile clusters within 4 meters of Bosc to host syrphid flies and minute pirate bugs feeding on pear psylla.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: Asian pear Hosui pairs with chamomile in the understory to draw parasitoid wasps that knock back pear psylla and aphid pressure.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications

entries.description: Calendula sown beneath Bing draws hoverflies and lacewings that suppress black cherry aphid colonies on new shoots.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications

entries.description: Calendula attracts predatory insects to Rainier; the sticky resins also trap small aphids that would otherwise reach the canopy.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: Montmorency tart cherry benefits from calendula plantings that draw syrphid flies and lacewings during the spring aphid peak.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension tree fruit IPM guide

entries.description: Tansy clusters around Elberta repel peach tree borer moths and attract parasitic wasps targeting oriental fruit moth.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: Establish 2 to 3 tansy plants at the Contender drip line for borer deterrence; cut blooms before seed set to prevent spread.
entries.source: USDA Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Tansy near Reliance peach deters borers and ants while attracting tachinid flies that parasitize oriental fruit moth larvae.
entries.source: USDA Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Plant Music garlic in a ring at the Stanley plum drip line; sulfur volatiles mask the fruit volatiles that plum curculio uses to find young plums.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'; USDA Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Music garlic around Italian Prune deters plum curculio adults and reduces aphid pressure on new growth in spring.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: A Music garlic ring at the Methley drip line confounds plum curculio host-finding and discourages aphid colonization of leaders.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: Damson plum pairs with Music garlic at the drip line; the allium volatiles reduce curculio damage and deter brown rot vectors.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: A thyme ground cover under Parfianka disrupts ant trails that farm sap suckers, while bloom feeds parasitoid wasps targeting leaffooted bug nymphs.
entries.source: USDA Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Thyme planted in the Angel Red drip line repels ants that protect aphid colonies and adds drought-tolerant ground cover.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: French summer thyme under Eversweet pomegranate deters ant farming and conserves soil moisture in arid plantings.
entries.source: USDA Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Plant Arp rosemary near Chicago Hardy fig to support early pollinators and deter root-knot nematode activity in the upper rhizosphere.
entries.source: Permaculture Designers Manual - Bill Mollison

entries.description: Plant Arp rosemary 1 meter from Celeste fig as an evergreen pollinator anchor and to discourage deer browsing on low fig branches.
entries.source: Permaculture Designers Manual - Bill Mollison

entries.description: Underplant Shenandoah pawpaw with ramps; the spring ephemeral occupies the same forest niche, harvests sunlight before leaf-out, and dies back before pawpaw fruit set.
entries.source: Toensmeier 'Edible Forest Gardens'

entries.description: Ramps thrive in the dappled shade under Overleese pawpaw and form a self-perpetuating spring crop that decays into the pawpaw root zone as mulch.
entries.source: Toensmeier 'Edible Forest Gardens'

entries.description: Plant ramp bulblets under Susquehanna pawpaw for a spring-ephemeral harvest that completes its cycle before the pawpaw canopy fully leafs out.
entries.source: Toensmeier 'Edible Forest Gardens'

entries.description: Ramps under Mango pawpaw occupy the early spring light window, suppress weeds, and add allium volatiles that may deter deer browsing the young trees.
entries.source: Toensmeier 'Edible Forest Gardens'

entries.description: Plant Regent serviceberry beside Adams elderberry for sequential bloom that supports native bees from early April through June, doubling pollinator visits in the guild.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications; Toensmeier 'Edible Forest Gardens'

entries.description: Smokey serviceberry blooms early; pairing with later-blooming York elderberry creates an extended pollinator corridor and bird forage hedge.
entries.source: Toensmeier 'Edible Forest Gardens'

entries.description: Thiessen serviceberry plus Bob Gordon elderberry stacks early and mid-season bloom to anchor native bee populations across the orchard floor.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications

entries.description: Underplant Red Lake currant 2 to 3 meters south of Illinois Everbearing mulberry; the dappled afternoon shade reduces currant leaf scorch and extends harvest in hot zones.
entries.source: Toensmeier 'Edible Forest Gardens'

entries.description: Black currant tolerates partial shade; planting it under Pakistan mulberry reduces summer sunscald on the currant and uses vertical space efficiently.
entries.source: Toensmeier 'Edible Forest Gardens'

entries.description: Poorman gooseberry placed in the partial shade of Illinois Everbearing mulberry stays cooler in midsummer, reducing powdery mildew pressure.
entries.source: Toensmeier 'Edible Forest Gardens'

entries.description: Titania currant under Black English mulberry benefits from cooler root zones and dappled light, extending fruit hang and reducing leaf drop.
entries.source: Toensmeier 'Edible Forest Gardens'

entries.description: Northern bayberry is a non-legume nitrogen fixer via actinorhizal symbiosis; planting it 2 meters from Jefferson hazelnut supplies steady nitrogen and supports native bees.
entries.source: Toensmeier 'Edible Forest Gardens'; Xerces Society

entries.description: Bayberry next to Yamhill hazelnut fixes atmospheric nitrogen and supports wild bee diversity, improving overall guild fertility without competing for canopy space.
entries.source: Toensmeier 'Edible Forest Gardens'

entries.description: Plant New Jersey tea (Ceanothus americanus) near Jefferson hazelnut; the actinorhizal shrub fixes nitrogen and feeds dozens of native bee species during hazelnut catkin set.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications; Toensmeier 'Edible Forest Gardens'

entries.description: Ceanothus thyrsiflorus next to Yamhill hazelnut adds nitrogen and supports a strong native bee community that improves catkin pollination and nut set.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications

entries.description: Plant Bocking 14 comfrey at the Fuyu drip line; chop and drop 2 to 3 times per season for potassium that improves fruit firmness and storage.
entries.source: Toensmeier 'Edible Forest Gardens'

entries.description: Bocking 14 comfrey under Hachiya mines deep potassium and calcium, reducing fruit cracking and feeding the tree as the foliage decays.
entries.source: Permaculture Designers Manual - Bill Mollison

entries.description: Plant comfrey at the Li jujube drip line as a chop-and-drop mulch source; it tolerates the dry soils jujube prefers and feeds the tree steadily.
entries.source: Toensmeier 'Edible Forest Gardens'

entries.description: Bocking 14 comfrey under Honey Jar jujube provides slow-release potassium and a living mulch that suppresses weeds in arid plantings.
entries.source: Permaculture Designers Manual - Bill Mollison

entries.description: Sea buckthorn fixes nitrogen via actinorhizal symbiosis; planting Viking aronia 2 meters downwind feeds the chokeberry and increases its fruit yield over time.
entries.source: Toensmeier 'Edible Forest Gardens'

entries.description: Ben Sarek currant placed near sea buckthorn benefits from the shrub's nitrogen contribution and partial wind protection in exposed plantings.
entries.source: Toensmeier 'Edible Forest Gardens'

entries.description: Plant borage at 1 plant per 3 strawberry plants; the constant bee traffic boosts Honeoye fruit set and reduces malformed berries from poor pollination.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications; Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: Borage interplanted with Jewel strawberries lifts pollinator visits, improves berry uniformity, and accumulates trace minerals when chopped as mulch.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications

entries.description: Plant borage along the south edge of an Earliglow bed; the early bloom synchronizes with strawberry flowering and brings bumblebees that improve fruit set.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications

entries.description: Borage near Seascape day-neutral strawberries supports continuous pollinator visits across the long Seascape bloom window for steady fruit quality.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications

entries.description: Plant borage at one corner of a Brandywine bed; bumblebee buzz pollination on borage attracts the same bees to large Brandywine flowers, lifting fruit set.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'; Xerces Society

entries.description: Borage adjacent to Cherokee Purple lifts bumblebee activity, improving buzz pollination of the large tomato flowers and reducing catfacing.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications

entries.description: Plant borage near Black Krim to attract bumblebees that buzz-pollinate the open flowers, reducing blossom drop in heat and improving fruit shape.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: Borage along a Sungold row keeps bumblebees in steady rotation, lifting pollinator pressure on the small Sungold flowers and improving cluster set.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications

entries.description: Sow a phacelia strip 3 meters from Honeycrisp 2 weeks before bloom; the overlap with apple bloom doubles bee visits and improves fruit set on king blossoms.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications; WSU Tree Fruit Research

entries.description: A phacelia strip near Cosmic Crisp drives strong honey bee and bumblebee visitation; time sowing 30 to 45 days before apple bloom to align peaks.
entries.source: WSU Tree Fruit Research

entries.description: Phacelia tanacetifolia between Gala rows attracts honey bees, bumblebees, and syrphid flies that pollinate apples and prey on aphids.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications

entries.description: Pear flowers are less attractive to bees than apple; planting phacelia near Bartlett pulls in pollinators and improves often-marginal pear fruit set.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications

entries.description: Sow Pacific Beauty calendula along Savoy cabbage rows; the sticky resin and flowers trap aphids and host hoverflies that prey on cabbage aphid colonies.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'; Xerces Society

entries.description: Pacific Beauty calendula edging a red cabbage bed pulls in syrphid flies that suppress cabbage aphid and Coccinellidae targeting flea beetles.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications

entries.description: Calendula at the edge of a Buttercrunch lettuce planting brings hoverflies and lacewings that prey on lettuce aphid colonies.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: Pacific Beauty calendula bordering a romaine block supports aphid predators and pulls flea beetles off the lettuce as a sacrificial bloom.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications

entries.description: Plant Jewel Mix nasturtium 1 meter from Delicata hills; aphids preferentially colonize the nasturtium and squash bugs lay fewer eggs on the cucurbit foliage.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'; USDA Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Jewel Mix nasturtium around Kabocha squash pulls aphids and acts as a sentinel for squash bug nymphs that are easier to spot on bare nasturtium stems.
entries.source: USDA Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Plant Jewel Mix nasturtium between Spaghetti squash plants as a living aphid trap; cull infested vines weekly to break the pest cycle.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: Hubbard squash is itself a classic squash bug trap; ringing it with Jewel Mix nasturtium adds an aphid trap layer and confuses cucumber beetles.
entries.source: USDA Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Jewel Mix nasturtium between Marketmore hills releases volatiles that deter striped and spotted cucumber beetles, reducing bacterial wilt transmission.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: Plant Jewel Mix nasturtium near Suyo Long cucumbers; the foliage volatiles disrupt cucumber beetle host finding on the long Asian-type vines.
entries.source: USDA Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Jewel Mix nasturtium intercropped with Straight Eight cucumbers reduces cucumber beetle pressure and acts as a sacrificial aphid host.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: Sow Mammoth sunflowers 3 weeks before pole beans so the 2 to 3 meter stalks form a living trellis; one stalk supports 2 to 3 bean vines through harvest.
entries.source: Three Sisters traditional planting; Toensmeier 'Edible Forest Gardens'

entries.description: Italian White sunflower stalks make a strong pole bean trellis; sow sunflowers 3 weeks before beans and thin to one stalk per 30 cm.
entries.source: Three Sisters traditional planting; Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: Cosmos Sensation along a Brandywine row hosts hoverflies and predatory wasps that suppress hornworms and aphids during fruit set.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications

entries.description: Jacob Cline bee balm near Brandywine pulls in bumblebees and hummingbirds; the bumblebee traffic improves buzz pollination on the large tomato flowers.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'; Xerces Society

entries.description: Jacob Cline planted at a Cherokee Purple bed corner attracts bumblebees for buzz pollination and lifts overall fruit set in heat.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications

entries.description: Lemon bee balm beside Sungold draws bumblebees and tachinid flies that parasitize tomato hornworms, layering pollination and biocontrol.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications

entries.description: Korean mint Agastache rugosa near Marketmore cucumbers brings honey bees and bumblebees that lift cucumber fruit set under hot conditions.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications

entries.description: Agastache rugosa near Delicata squash hosts squash bees and bumblebees and feeds parasitic wasps targeting squash vine borer.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications

entries.description: Hummingbird mint near Kabocha squash pulls in hummingbirds and long-tongued bees, with bloom timing matching cucurbit flowering.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator publications

entries.description: Avoid interplanting Brandywine with Savoy cabbage; tomato root exudates inhibit brassica growth and the two crops compete heavily for nitrogen and calcium.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'; USDA Cooperative Extension allelopathy publications

entries.description: Cherokee Purple suppresses neighboring red cabbage growth through root exudates and shading; keep at least 1.5 meters apart or rotate the bed.
entries.source: USDA Cooperative Extension allelopathy publications

entries.description: Sungold and purple sprouting broccoli both demand heavy nitrogen and calcium; co-planting causes stunted broccoli heads and blossom end rot on tomatoes.
entries.source: Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: Tomato root exudates suppress kale germination and early growth; site Black Krim and Redbor kale in separate beds for best yields of both.
entries.source: USDA Cooperative Extension allelopathy publications

entries.description: Onion sulfur compounds inhibit Rhizobium nodulation on Provider bush bean roots, reducing nitrogen fixation and stunting bean growth.
entries.source: USDA Cooperative Extension allelopathy publications; Riotte 'Carrots Love Tomatoes'

entries.description: Yellow storage onion exudes thiosulfinates that suppress Rhizobium colonization of Kentucky Wonder roots; plant alliums and beans in separate beds.
entries.source: USDA Cooperative Extension allelopathy publications

entries.description: Walla Walla onion inhibits nitrogen fixation in Jade bush bean by suppressing Rhizobium; expect yellow leaves and reduced pod set when intercropped.
entries.source: USDA Cooperative Extension allelopathy publications

entries.description: Music garlic releases sulfur volatiles that suppress Rhizobium nodulation on Blue Lake pole bean roots; rotate alliums away from bean beds.
entries.source: USDA Cooperative Extension allelopathy publications

entries.description: German Extra Hardy garlic inhibits Rhizobium on Contender bush bean roots, cutting nitrogen fixation and reducing pod yield by 20 to 30 percent.
entries.source: USDA Cooperative Extension allelopathy publications

entries.description: Black walnut releases juglone from roots and leaf litter that wilts Brandywine within days; keep tomato beds at least 25 meters from the walnut drip line.
entries.source: USDA Cooperative Extension allelopathy publications; Toensmeier 'Edible Forest Gardens'

entries.description: Juglone from black walnut kills Cherokee Purple plants; site tomato rows well outside the walnut root zone, which extends 1.5 times the canopy radius.
entries.source: USDA Cooperative Extension allelopathy publications

entries.description: Jalapeno peppers wilt and die within the black walnut root zone; juglone is highly toxic to Solanaceae and persists in soil for years after tree removal.
entries.source: USDA Cooperative Extension allelopathy publications

entries.description: Jimmy Nardello sweet pepper roots are highly sensitive to juglone; keep peppers at least 25 meters from black walnut and avoid mulching with walnut leaves or chips.
entries.source: USDA Cooperative Extension allelopathy publications

entries.description: Bluecrop blueberry decline near black walnut is caused by juglone; site blueberries well outside the walnut root zone and never compost walnut leaves into blueberry beds.
entries.source: USDA Cooperative Extension allelopathy publications

entries.description: Patriot blueberry stunts and dies in juglone-contaminated soil from black walnut; minimum 25 meter buffer between the species is required.
entries.source: USDA Cooperative Extension allelopathy publications

entries.description: Mammoth sunflower roots release chlorogenic and caffeic acids that suppress potato tuber initiation; keep sunflowers and potato beds at least 3 meters apart.
entries.source: USDA Cooperative Extension allelopathy publications

entries.description: Oilseed sunflower residues inhibit potato sprouting and tuber growth; do not rotate potato into a recent sunflower bed without 1 season of cover crop in between.
entries.source: USDA Cooperative Extension allelopathy publications

entries.description: Plant crimson clover Dixie in fall, terminate at flowering 2 weeks before transplanting tomato. Releases 70 to 100 lb N per acre and forms in-place mulch that suppresses early weeds.
entries.source: USDA-SARE Managing Cover Crops Profitably 3rd ed

entries.description: Crimson clover Frontier overwinters in zone 8 Gulf coast then dies back at bloom, leaving residue that feeds heat-loving Creole tomato through its first month of fruit set.
entries.source: LSU AgCenter cover crop trials

entries.description: USDA-ARS Beltsville no-till trials documented hairy vetch mulch supplying full N for tomato and reducing early blight severity by sustaining canopy humidity less than bare ground.
entries.source: USDA-ARS Abdul-Baki and Teasdale Beltsville no-till tomato

entries.description: Roll-crimp crimson clover at bloom and transplant jalapeno through residue. The slow N release matches pepper demand and the mulch buffers root-zone temperatures.
entries.source: Penn State Cover Crop Council vegetable rotations

entries.description: Anaheim peppers transplanted into terminated crimson clover residue benefit from steady N mineralization through July fruit set without supplemental fertilizer.
entries.source: UMass Vegetable Program cover crop guide

entries.description: Hairy vetch terminated at full bloom contributes 90 to 150 lb N per acre, covering most of poblano season demand. Plant pepper through rolled residue for living mulch effect.
entries.source: USDA-SARE Managing Cover Crops Profitably

entries.description: Austrian winter pea winter-kills in zone 6 and lower, leaving an easy-to-incorporate N-rich residue ideal for spring pepper beds. Decomposes faster than vetch.
entries.source: Penn State Cover Crop Council legume cover crops

entries.description: Drill Austrian winter pea Arvense in September, terminate at flowering. Fast decomposition feeds heavy-feeding tomato during its first six weeks after transplant.
entries.source: USDA-SARE Managing Cover Crops Profitably

entries.description: Fall-planted Austrian winter pea winter-kills and the N-rich residue is ready for early spring cabbage transplant. Avoids the carbon-N imbalance of cereal residues.
entries.source: UMass Vegetable Program brassica rotation

entries.description: Summer cowpea cover terminated late August fixes 100 to 150 lb N per acre, supplying fall broccoli with steady N without the lodging risk of vetch.
entries.source: USDA-SARE Southern Cover Crops Council

entries.description: Mississippi Silver cowpea cover terminated in August leaves enough N and friable soil for fall savoy cabbage transplant. Works in zone 7 and warmer.
entries.source: USDA-SARE Southern Cover Crops Council

entries.description: Pink Eye Purple Hull cowpea grown as summer cover contributes 80 to 120 lb N per acre. Terminate at flower then direct seed or transplant fall cauliflower.
entries.source: USDA-SARE Southern Cover Crops Council

entries.description: Mow Ladino white clover low and transplant kale into strips. Living mulch suppresses weeds, fixes N in-place, and shelters ground beetles that eat cabbage looper eggs.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension living mulch trials

entries.description: New Zealand white clover used as living mulch in broccoli alleys reduces flea beetle pressure and fixes 50 to 80 lb N per acre over the season.
entries.source: UVM Vegetable and Berry Program living mulch

entries.description: Plant buckwheat Mancan between strawberry rows in renovation year. Mobilizes phosphorus, smothers weeds in 30 days, and flowers feed pollinators for early bloom.
entries.source: USDA-SARE Managing Cover Crops Profitably

entries.description: Sow buckwheat Mancan as quick summer break between Seascape strawberry plantings. Phosphorus mobilization and weed smothering set up better fall transplant establishment.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension strawberry rotation

entries.description: Buckwheat Koban sown after Honeoye renovation grows in 28 days, mobilizes soil phosphorus, and is tilled in before fall planting. Reduces weed pressure year over year.
entries.source: USDA-SARE Managing Cover Crops Profitably

entries.description: Buckwheat used as a short summer break between onion plantings smothers pigweed and lambsquarters within 30 days, then turns under to leave loose seedbed for fall sets.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension onion IPM

entries.description: Short 6 week buckwheat catch crop ahead of fall-planted Walla Walla onions outcompetes summer annuals and breaks down quickly enough to avoid tying up nitrogen.
entries.source: Oregon State Extension onion production

entries.description: Mustard cover crop incorporated at full bloom releases isothiocyanates that suppress Verticillium dahliae and Pratylenchus nematodes in the following potato crop.
entries.source: ATTRA Sustainable Agriculture biofumigation

entries.description: Biofumigant mustard rotated ahead of potato onion plots reduces white rot and scab through glucosinolate breakdown when the residue is flail-mowed and incorporated immediately.
entries.source: ATTRA Sustainable Agriculture biofumigation

entries.description: Tillage radish drilled in late summer breaks compaction with a 2 to 3 ft taproot and winter-kills, leaving channels that improve potato tuber expansion the following spring.
entries.source: Penn State Cover Crop Council tillage radish

entries.description: Daikon-type tillage radish breaks plow pan ahead of carrot beds, giving straight long-rooted carrots like Imperator and Touchon better cell penetration the next season.
entries.source: USDA-SARE Managing Cover Crops Profitably

entries.description: Imperator carrots need deep friable soil. Tillage radish the prior summer pulls subsoil nutrients up and leaves vertical channels that prevent forked or stubby roots.
entries.source: Penn State Cover Crop Council tillage radish

entries.description: Sudangrass Piper releases sorgoleone, an allelopathic root exudate that suppresses pigweed and lambsquarters before slow-emerging carrot St Valery seedlings.
entries.source: USDA-ARS allelopathy publications sorgoleone

entries.description: Mow-killed sudangrass residue suppresses early-season weeds in direct-seeded beet plots and adds 4 to 6 tons of biomass that improves beet root expansion.
entries.source: USDA-ARS allelopathy publications sorgoleone

entries.description: Parsnip is slow to germinate so weed pressure is critical. Sudangrass Piper terminated the prior summer leaves allelopathic residues that suppress weeds for 4 to 6 weeks.
entries.source: USDA-SARE Managing Cover Crops Profitably

entries.description: Sunn hemp planted as a summer cover in zone 5 and warmer fixes 120 to 180 lb N per acre in 60 days. Terminate at 4 ft for residue ahead of next-year tomato.
entries.source: University of Florida IFAS sunn hemp guide

entries.description: Sunn hemp summer cover ahead of fall or following-spring bell pepper supplies N and suppresses root-knot nematodes through nonhost effect documented in IFAS trials.
entries.source: University of Florida IFAS sunn hemp guide

entries.description: Strip-cropped alfalfa in orchard middles fixes N at depth and brings calcium up via taproots. Mow flowers to feed pollinators in early summer between fruit-set passes.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension orchard floor management

entries.description: Magnum alfalfa as orchard floor cover between Liberty apple rows fixes 100 to 200 lb N per acre and the deep taproots cycle subsoil minerals into surface litter.
entries.source: Penn State Cover Crop Council orchard covers

entries.description: Berseem clover Bigbee winter-grown in Florida and Gulf coast supplies 100 lb N per acre and terminates easily ahead of spring tomato transplant.
entries.source: University of Florida IFAS berseem clover

entries.description: Frosty berseem tolerates light freezes so it holds biomass through Florida winter, then terminates at bloom for in-place N mulch for spring-planted tomato.
entries.source: University of Florida IFAS berseem clover

entries.description: Balansa clover Fixation grown on orchard floor provides bloom-time N and a dense low cover that conserves moisture. Mow short before harvest for clean drops.
entries.source: Oregon State Extension orchard floor management

entries.description: Balansa clover Fixation as orchard floor cover under tart cherry fixes N at depth and supports early-spring pollinators that visit cherry bloom.
entries.source: Xerces Society orchard pollinator research

entries.description: Soil builder mix combining legume and small grain components leaves a 50:50 N and biomass balance that decomposes evenly under tomato over a 12 week fruiting window.
entries.source: USDA-SARE Managing Cover Crops Profitably mixes

entries.description: Summer smother mix of sorghum sudangrass and cowpea grown June through August suppresses weeds and supplies N ahead of fall-transplanted broccoli.
entries.source: USDA-SARE Managing Cover Crops Profitably mixes

entries.description: Sorghum sudangrass component of summer smother mix releases sorgoleone for weed suppression. Cowpea component supplies N for fall cabbage transplant.
entries.source: USDA-SARE Managing Cover Crops Profitably mixes

entries.description: Pollinator mix sown in orchard alleys provides sequential bloom that supports native bees from apple petal-fall through summer, improving fruit set and natural pest control.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator research

entries.description: Pollinator mix on cherry orchard floor sustains mason bees and bumblebees during the short cherry bloom window, increasing fruit set 10 to 20 percent in trials.
entries.source: Xerces Society orchard pollinator research

entries.description: Genovese basil interplanted at 2 ft spacing among Arkansas Traveler tomatoes suppresses thrips and whitefly populations and shares similar water and warmth preferences.
entries.source: Cunningham Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Thai basil emits methyl chavicol that confuses tomato hornworm moths searching for oviposition sites. Plant on tomato row ends and at 3 ft intervals.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension IPM guides

entries.description: Spicy globe basil forms compact 12 inch domes that fit between bell pepper plants. Volatile oils mask pepper scent from aphids and thrips while attracting hover flies.
entries.source: Riotte Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Plant 2 spicy globe basil between each pair of jalapeno plants. Repels aphids without competing for vertical space and tolerates the same hot, full-sun bed.
entries.source: Plant Anywhere companion matrix

entries.description: Cinnamon basil interplanted at the corners of poblano beds attracts beneficial wasps that parasitize pepper hornworms and reduces aphid colonies on new growth.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension IPM guides

entries.description: Jewel mix nasturtium planted on the perimeter of cucumber rows pulls cucumber beetles and squash bugs to itself, sparing the crop. Pull infested plants weekly to break the cycle.
entries.source: Cunningham Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Empress of India nasturtium concentrates aphid colonies on its own stems, sparing winter squash like Delicata. Crush or remove infested plants every 2 weeks.
entries.source: Cunningham Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Climbing Spitfire nasturtium trained up squash trellises traps squash bugs and aphids while attracting hover flies and predatory wasps to the canopy.
entries.source: Plant Anywhere companion matrix

entries.description: Alaska variegated nasturtium on cucumber bed edges traps cucumber beetles. The light variegated foliage is also reported to deter striped cucumber beetle visual searching.
entries.source: Cunningham Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Common chive interplanted in carrot rows masks carrot scent from carrot rust fly. Allow chives to bloom for hover flies that eat aphids on carrot tops.
entries.source: Riotte Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Garlic chive Staro releases sulfur compounds that deter carrot rust fly oviposition. Plant a continuous edging along Danvers carrot rows for season-long protection.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension carrot IPM

entries.description: Edge Chantenay carrot beds with Staro garlic chive to mask the volatile compounds carrot rust fly uses to locate hosts. Sulfur compounds also deter aphids.
entries.source: Riotte Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Kentucky Colonel spearmint emits pulegone and menthone that confuse cabbage moth host-finding. Grow in buried pots at cabbage bed corners to contain spread.
entries.source: Cunningham Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Mojito mint volatile oils repel cabbage looper and imported cabbage worm. Sink containers between broccoli plants so mint roots stay contained but foliage scents the bed.
entries.source: Riotte Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Kentucky Colonel spearmint between kale plants suppresses flea beetle and cabbage moth searching. Use container barriers because spearmint runners will overrun a kale bed.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension brassica IPM

entries.description: Greek oregano True forms low spreading mats that shade pepper root zones and emit carvacrol, which deters aphids and spider mites. Trim to keep airflow at pepper canopy.
entries.source: Plant Anywhere companion matrix

entries.description: Plant Greek oregano True as ground cover under indeterminate tomato. Carvacrol-rich volatiles deter aphids and the dense mat conserves soil moisture during fruit set.
entries.source: Cunningham Great Garden Companions

entries.description: French summer thyme releases thymol that deters cabbage moth oviposition. Edge cabbage beds with thyme clumps on 18 inch spacing for continuous chemical barrier.
entries.source: Riotte Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Lemon thyme citral and thymol mask cabbage scent. Interplant 1 thyme per 4 red cabbage and prune to a compact shape so it does not shade lower cabbage leaves.
entries.source: Cunningham Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Plant lemon thyme on broccoli bed corners to deter cabbage moth and flea beetle. The citrus-thyme blend of volatiles is effective from emergence through head harvest.
entries.source: Plant Anywhere companion matrix

entries.description: Berggarten sage forms a 2 ft mound of thick aromatic leaves that deters cabbage moth and carrot fly. Plant on bed corners where its size will not crowd cabbage.
entries.source: Cunningham Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Purple Icterina sage emits camphor and thujone that deter cabbage looper and imported cabbageworm moths around savoy cabbage. Compact habit fits between transplants.
entries.source: Riotte Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Arp rosemary is hardy to zone 6 and provides persistent year-round volatile defense against cabbage moth. Trim back annually to keep within cabbage bed footprint.
entries.source: Cunningham Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Tuscan Blue rosemary planted at Provider bush bean bed corners repels Mexican bean beetle adults seeking oviposition sites. Volatile camphor compounds peak in midsummer.
entries.source: Riotte Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Plant Tuscan Blue rosemary at the base of Blue Lake pole bean trellis. Camphor-rich volatiles deter bean beetle and rust mite from climbing into the canopy.
entries.source: Cunningham Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Summer savory interplanted with Contender bush beans deters Mexican bean beetle and bean weevil. Classic European companion that also enhances bean flavor at table.
entries.source: Riotte Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Summer savory at the base of Kentucky Wonder pole bean trellis deters bean beetle. Easy to interplant since both prefer warm soil and full sun.
entries.source: Cunningham Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Winter savory perennial clumps at bean bed edges deter bean beetle year over year. Less aggressive in spread than summer savory and hardy through zone 5.
entries.source: Plant Anywhere companion matrix

entries.description: Blue Fortune anise hyssop draws bumblebees and parasitic wasps that attack cabbage worm larvae. Plant on cabbage bed corners for season-long predator support.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator research

entries.description: Pennyroyal edges around strawberry beds deter ants that farm aphids on strawberry crowns. Caution: pennyroyal oil is toxic to pets and pregnant gardeners.
entries.source: Riotte Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Plant pennyroyal as low edging around Honeoye strawberry beds. Pulegone volatiles deter ants and the spreading mat suppresses weeds between crowns.
entries.source: Cunningham Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Catnip nepetalactone deters cucumber beetle and flea beetle when grown around cucumber beds. Plant 1 catnip per 6 ft of cucumber row but mow before seed-set.
entries.source: Iowa State University catnip pest deterrence

entries.description: Catnip volatile nepetalactone is 10x more effective than DEET at repelling cucumber beetle in Iowa State trials. Use container plantings near pickling cucumber beds.
entries.source: Iowa State University Junwei Zhu catnip research

entries.description: Tansy planted at potato bed corners repels Colorado potato beetle adults. Cut flowers before seed-set as tansy self-sows aggressively and naturalizes.
entries.source: Riotte Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Sweet Annie Artemisia emits camphor and artemisia ketone that deter cabbage moth and flea beetle. Plant on bed perimeter as it grows 4 to 6 ft tall.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension medicinal herbs

entries.description: Let dill flower among Diva parthenocarpic cucumber to attract braconid wasps that parasitize cucumber beetle and aphid populations. Diva does not need pollinators itself.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension cucurbit IPM

entries.description: Dill seed type allowed to bolt and flower among Armenian cucumber attracts pollinators and predatory wasps. Cucumber and dill share light and water needs.
entries.source: Riotte Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Red shiso emits perillaldehyde that deters small leafhoppers and aphids. Traditional Japanese tomato companion that also makes a useful kitchen herb for pickling.
entries.source: Japanese Society for Horticultural Science perilla companion

entries.description: Green shiso interplanted with cucumber repels small leafhoppers and serves as a heat-tolerant edible companion. Plant at 18 inch spacing on cucumber bed edges.
entries.source: Japanese Society for Horticultural Science perilla companion

entries.description: Epazote interplanted with Contender bush bean deters bean beetle and adds a culinary herb traditionally cooked with bean dishes to reduce flatulence.
entries.source: Cunningham Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Blue borage attracts predatory wasps that parasitize tomato hornworm. Borage flowers also signal bumblebees to visit nearby tomato blossoms for better fruit set.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator research

entries.description: Tomato and bell pepper share Verticillium, early blight, and tobacco mosaic virus. Avoid same-bed interplanting and rotate at minimum 3 years apart on the same ground.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension nightshade rotation

entries.description: Same Solanaceae family. Shared susceptibility to Verticillium, root knot nematode, and Colorado potato beetle. Rotate at minimum 3 years apart.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension nightshade rotation

entries.description: Tomato and potato share Phytophthora infestans late blight. A single infected volunteer potato can wipe out a tomato planting. Do not plant in the same season or adjacent beds.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension late blight management

entries.description: Eggplant and pepper share Verticillium wilt and root-knot nematode. Avoid same-bed interplanting and rotate on a 3 year minimum cycle.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension nightshade rotation

entries.description: Capsicum annuum cultivars cross-pollinate freely. Plant jalapeno and Anaheim at least 300 ft apart if seed-saving to avoid surprise heat-level shifts.
entries.source: Seed Savers Exchange seed saving guide

entries.description: Bell pepper Capsicum annuum and habanero Capsicum chinense can cross-pollinate. Plant at minimum 500 ft apart if seed-saving or use mesh bags on flowering branches.
entries.source: Seed Savers Exchange seed saving guide

entries.description: Both Brassica oleracea cultivars share cabbage moth, flea beetle, clubroot, and downy mildew. Avoid concentrating brassicas in the same bed in the same season.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension brassica rotation

entries.description: Both Brassica oleracea. Same cabbage moth and clubroot pressure. Concentrating both in one bed accelerates pest buildup and tightens rotation requirements.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension brassica rotation

entries.description: Both Brassica oleracea. Shared cabbage moth, aphid, and clubroot pressure. Better to space across separate beds and rotate across the garden on a 3 year cycle.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension brassica rotation

entries.description: Onion root exudates suppress Rhizobium activity on pole bean roots, hurting N fixation in Kentucky Wonder. Keep separate beds or 3 ft buffer.
entries.source: Cunningham Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Fennel root and shoot exudates inhibit germination and growth of most garden vegetables including beans. Plant fennel in dedicated bed or container.
entries.source: Cunningham Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Florence fennel suppresses tomato growth through allelopathic root exudates. Keep at minimum 6 ft from tomato beds or grow in dedicated container.
entries.source: Cunningham Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Bronze fennel and carrot are both Apiaceae and can cross-pollinate. Fennel also allelopathically suppresses carrot. Plant fennel in a separate bed.
entries.source: Cunningham Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Anaheim and other Capsicum annuum are juglone-sensitive. Symptoms include wilting and rapid death even at low juglone levels in soil.
entries.source: USDA-ARS juglone allelopathy publications

entries.description: Tomato is the most juglone-sensitive garden crop. Single exposure to black walnut leaf litter or root contact causes rapid wilt and death. Keep beds 60+ ft from canopy.
entries.source: Penn State Extension juglone tolerance

entries.description: Sunflower head residue and root exudates inhibit potato growth in close interplanting. Velvet Queen branching sunflowers also shade low-growing potato canopy.
entries.source: USDA-ARS allelopathy publications sunflower

entries.description: Cereal rye must decompose for at least 3 weeks before tomato transplant. Fresh rye residue ties up nitrogen and releases allelopathic compounds that stunt tomato seedlings.
entries.source: USDA-SARE Managing Cover Crops Profitably

entries.description: Tillage radish decomposes in spring and ties up nitrogen for 3 to 5 weeks. If incorporated too late, heavy-feeding sweet corn shows N deficiency early in tassel formation.
entries.source: Penn State Cover Crop Council tillage radish

entries.description: Rye Aroostook releases benzoxazinoids and ties up surface N during decomposition. Wait 3 weeks after termination before direct-seeding sweet corn or supplement N.
entries.source: USDA-SARE Managing Cover Crops Profitably
